workspace.title: Choose a workspace
workspace.description: Your PageCrawl account has access to more than one workspace. Pick the one to bring into Home Assistant. To add another workspace, run this setup again.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate with PageCrawl
reauth_confirm.description: Your PageCrawl access has expired or was revoked. Sign in again to reconnect this workspace.

abort.already_configured: This workspace is already set up.
abort.oauth_error: Received an invalid response while signing in to PageCrawl.
abort.oauth_failed: Could not get an access token from PageCrawl.
abort.oauth_timeout: Timed out while waiting for PageCrawl authorization.
abort.missing_configuration: The integration is not configured. Add an Application Credential first.
abort.authorize_url_timeout: Timed out generating the authorize URL.
abort.no_url_available: No URL is available for the OAuth redirect. See the documentation for help.
abort.cannot_connect: Could not reach PageCrawl or read your account. Check your connection and try again.
abort.no_workspaces: No workspaces were found on your PageCrawl account.
abort.unknown_workspace: The selected workspace could not be found.
abort.wrong_account: The signed-in PageCrawl account does not match the workspace being reauthenticated.

init.title: PageCrawl options
init.description: Choose how this workspace receives updates. Push delivers changes the moment PageCrawl detects them but needs a URL that PageCrawl can reach (a Home Assistant Cloud subscription works automatically). Polling only checks on a schedule and works on local-only installs.

data_description.update_mode: Auto enables push when a reachable URL is available and otherwise polls. Push and poll forces push with a slow reconcile poll. Polling only never registers a webhook.
data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll, in seconds. Minimum 60. With push enabled this is only a slow reconciliation loop.
data_description.folder: Limit this workspace to a single PageCrawl folder by its slug. Leave empty for all folders.
data_description.tags: Limit to monitors with these tags, comma separated. Leave empty for all monitors.

application_credentials.description: PageCrawl uses built-in credentials, so you do not need to add anything here.

check_now.name: Check now
check_now.description: Trigger an immediate check of one or more PageCrawl monitors, then refresh their entities.

entity_id.name: Entities
entity_id.description: Entities belonging to the monitors to check.

track_page.name: Track a new page
track_page.description: Create a new monitor in PageCrawl. Editing and removing monitors is done in the PageCrawl web app.

url.name: URL
url.description: The web address to start monitoring.

name.name: Name
name.description: A friendly name for the monitor. Defaults to the page title.

tracking_mode.name: Tracking mode
tracking_mode.description: What to track on the page. Leave empty to track the full page.

selector.name: CSS selector
selector.description: For text or number tracking, the CSS selector of the element to watch.

prompt.name: AI prompt
prompt.description: For AI extract tracking, the instruction describing what to pull from the page.

frequency.name: Frequency
frequency.description: How often PageCrawl rechecks the page.

entry_id.name: Workspace (advanced)
entry_id.description: The workspace to create the monitor in. Only needed when more than one PageCrawl workspace is set up.

push_unavailable.title: PageCrawl push updates unavailable
push_unavailable.description: PageCrawl could not register push updates for {workspace} because no externally reachable URL is available. This workspace is now using polling. To enable instant updates, connect Home Assistant Cloud or expose a reachable URL, then reload the integration.
